evidence: Module header 'Optimal monotone families for the discrete isoperimetric inequality'; the statement asks when the isoperimetric inequality (IR) on the discrete cube is sharp up to a constant.
Docstring: 'This was a missing lemma in the work of Kahn and Kalai... The related Kahn–Kalai conjecture was settled by Park and Pham.'
Docstring: 'Conjecture 7 from Kahn–Kalai 2006', adding the critical-probability assumption mu_t(F)=1/2 to the original statement.
The variant asks for the same IsOptimal conclusion, i.e. sharpness of the discrete isoperimetric inequality for a monotone family.
Docstring: 'Weaker version proven by Kahn–Kalai: the same conclusion holds when 1000 log n is replaced by C_eps n^eps.'
The variant asserts the IsOptimal conclusion (sharpness of the discrete isoperimetric inequality) under a weaker interval condition.
Module header: 'Does the 6-sphere S^6 admit the structure of a complex manifold?'; the statement asks for a holomorphic atlas making S^6 a complex manifold.
The statement is about the unit 6-sphere, defined via unitSphere n as Metric.sphere 0 1 in Euclidean space.
Docstring: 'Is there any polynomial f(x, y) in Q[x, y] such that f : Q x Q -> Q is a bijection?'
The question asks whether the evaluation map of a bivariate rational polynomial can be a bijection Q x Q -> Q (Function.Bijective).
The polynomial has rational coefficients and the map is Q x Q -> Q; the problem is specifically about the rational numbers.
Docstring: 'Does the connectedness of f imply that of f^{-1}?' where a connected map sends connected sets to connected sets (IsConnectedMap).
Docstring: 'Assume for n>1, f : R^n -> R^n is a bijection'; the statement quantifies over equivalences f : R^n ~ R^n.
Docstring: 'There exists a connected bijection R -> R^2 where the inverse is not connected' (IsConnectedMap of f but not f.symm).
Docstring: 'Does there exist a category that is pretriangulated but not triangulated?' (Pretriangulated vs IsTriangulated).
Module header: 'Why do polynomials with coefficients 0,1 like to have only factors with 0,1 coefficients?'; the statement is about monic real polynomials whose product is a 0,1 polynomial.
Docstring: 'Can a unit square be covered by rectangles of width 1/(n+1) and height 1/(n+2)?'
The covering question is the covering counterpart of the classic 1/k x 1/(k+1) rectangle packing problem stated alongside it in the same file.
Docstring: 'can a unit square be packed with rectangles of width 1/(n+1) and height 1/(n+2)?' with Configuration.IsPacking requiring disjoint interiors.
The packing target is the unit square (unitSquare = {p | 0 <= p.1 <= 1, 0 <= p.2 <= 1}).
Docstring: 'It is known that packing the rectangles into a square of side length 133/132 is possible.'
Docstring: 'It is known that packing the rectangles into a square of side length 501/500 is possible.'
Docstring: 'Is it true that sup f(x) = sup f(x + grad f(x))?' for C^1 functions; the statement uses gradient f.
The conjecture equates two suprema over R^n (iSup in EReal) and the boundedness above of the two ranges.
Docstring: 'Is the boundedness of sup f(x) and sup f(x + grad f(x)) equivalent?'; the perturbation is by the gradient of f.
Docstring: 'Does the equality sup f(x) = sup f(x + grad f(x)) hold when both suprema are finite?'
The variant asserts equality of the two suprema under the assumption that both ranges are bounded above.
